violations.text: (Shrimp), which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC voluntarily disposed of food at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024

(Rice bowls with fish and chicken) was observed held at 120 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, food was made and packaged under an hour prior to inspection. Food was reheated over 165 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Shrimp was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Cooling methods and procedures reviewed with PIC at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024
